solutions.solution: From statement I, n, the number of sides, can be found by using the formula: Sum of angles = 540° = 90° × (2n - 4). From Statement II, n, the number of sides, can be found by using the formula: n = 360°/angle subtended by anyone side
From the two statements, all that can be inferred is that the colour of his eye is not blue. But the actual colour can't be found.

Together, the two statements are sufficient to get an answer. We know that we can find the day of any date if we know the day of any other date. So, the day falling on 2nd April, 1995, can be found by using statement I. Then, the day falling on 3rd April can be found out.

The two statements are not sufficient to guarantee that he never misses his office. A possibility remains that he goes to some third place and is never fined for absence because of the simple fact that there is no provision of fine for absence in his office (!). Who knows!
Using syllogistic reasoning (Rule: A + A = A), we find "All basketballs are footballs." But "All footballs are basketballs" is neither proved nor disproved.
Using syllogistic reasoning, we get A + I = no conclusion. Further, statement I talks about 'sports layers' not 'sportsmen'.

Both the statements are independently sufficient. From statement I, Tendulkar's score is (80 × 52 - 79 × 50) which is definitely more than 100. (You do not need to find out the actual answer. See, the note after the answer to Q. 46.) From II, Manjrekar scored 99. Tendulkar scored more; so, he must have hit a century.
